---
title: "![](images/icons/icon_11.png){.title-icon} Comorbidity"
subtitle: "Charlson, multimorbidity and other comorbidity measures as covariates"
date: last-modified
---

::: {.callout-warning}
**Under development.** Structural outline — to be expanded with concrete examples.
:::

Comorbidity at baseline is a common covariate. It is derived from diagnoses (LPR) up to the index date and summarised in an index. Use a **ready-made algorithm** rather than coding it from scratch — see [Algorithms & special packages](14a_pakker-oversigt.qmd).

## Two routes

- **NMI (multimorbidity index):** see [NMI](14c_nmi.qmd).
- **Charlson Comorbidity Index:** the `comorbidity` package (Gasparini) maps ICD-10 → Charlson/Elixhauser score. *(Confirm the package on your project.)*

Both take your LPR diagnoses (filtered to the cohort and to the time **before index**) and return a score per `pnr`, which you save as `.rds` and join in [Phase 12](11_data-manipulation.qmd).

```r
cohort_pnrs <- unique(readRDS("datasets/full_cohort.rds")$pnr)

diagnoses_before_index <- open_dataset("path/to/lpr_diag/") %>%
  rename_with(tolower) %>%
  filter(pnr %in% !!cohort_pnrs) %>%      # ONLY your cohort
  # ... join date, keep diagnoses before index_date ...
  collect()
# → pass the diagnoses to comorbidity::comorbidity() / NMI to get a score per pnr
```

## See also
- [Algorithms & special packages](14a_pakker-oversigt.qmd) · [NMI](14c_nmi.qmd)
- [Phase 12 — Assemble and prepare the dataset](11_data-manipulation.qmd)
